reasoning: France are the clear market favorites at around 15/8 after an unbeaten run, with Mbappé in Golden Boot form and a favorable path opened up by the shock exits of Germany and the Netherlands on their side of the draw. Spain are the biggest threat — European champions on a 35-match unbeaten streak who have yet to concede a goal all tournament — but they face a stiffer bracket, meeting Belgium next and likely France in the semis. Argentina, the reigning champions, have survived scares (needing extra time versus Cape Verde and a late comeback from 2-0 down against Egypt) and share the softer bottom half of the draw with England, Norway and Switzerland, making them the likeliest finalist opposite France. Norway are the live dark horse after Haaland knocked out Brazil, sitting in the weaker half where a run to the final is realistic. The main risks to France are Spain's relentless form and the ever-present knockout resilience of Messi's Argentina.
